fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer occupational therapist license to colorado, occupational therapist compact colorado, colorado occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Colorado DORA — Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Colorado.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Complete an Accredited Occupational Therapy Program
steps.description: Graduate from an occupational therapy program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Occupational Therapy Education (ACOTE).

steps.title: Pass the NBCOT Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination.

steps.title: Apply for a Colorado Occupational Therapy License
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA) along with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Colorado OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Colorado has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Colorado
hero.dek: Colorado Occupational Therapy License requires graduation from an ACOTE-accredited program, passing the NBCOT exam, and a background check.
